lines.text: Monica, Chandler, and Phoebe's, Chandler, Joey, Rachel, and Phoebe are standing around the table drinking champagne as Ross enters.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey, what's going on?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hey.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I found a note on my door, "Come to Monica's quick, bring champagne and a Three Musketeers bar."

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (grabbing the candy bar) Yeah I'll take that.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What's up?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Monica and I are engaged.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh my God. (Hugs Chandler.) Congratulations.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Thanks.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Where is she?
[Cut to Monica out on the balcony.]

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (yelling at the top of her lungs) I'm engaged!!!!!! I'm engaged!!!!
[Cut back inside.]

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah, she's been out there for twenty minutes, I'm surprised you didn't hear her on the way over.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh, I thought it was just a kid yelling, "I'm gay! I'm gay!" Can I bring her in?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh no, let her stay out there. It's sweet.
[Cut to Monica.]

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I'm getting married!!!! I'm gonna be a bride!!!! (Someone else yells at her.) No, I will not shut up because I'm engaged! (He yells again.) Ohh, big talk! Huh, why don't you come over here and say that to me?! Huh, buddy?! Yeah, my fiancee will kick your ass! (Chandler starts to look worried.) Come on, apartment 20! Apartment 20!
[Cut back inside.]

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (To Ross) Okay, you get her in here. (To Joey) You bolt the door. I'll be in the closet.
Opening Credits
Monica, Chandler, and Phoebe's, continued from earlier, everyone is now in the living room drinking champagne.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Okay! Wait-wait-wait! Shhh! (Bangs on her class with a spoon to make a toast.) Okay, umm, I just wanna say that...I love you guys so-so much and-and thank you for being here on my special night. (Chandler clears his throat.) Our special night. I mean it just wouldn't be my-our-our night, if you all weren't here to celebrate with me-us-Damnit!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: It's okay, I want this to be your night too. (Raises his class.) To Monica.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Awww, come on-wait-stop it. Okay, to Monica.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: To Monica!
They all say to Monica, clink their glasses, and drink.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: So have you decided on a band for the wedding? Because, y'know, I'm kinda musical.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah Pheebs, honey, she just got engaged a couple of hours ago. I doubt she's even had time to...

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Well speaking of chiming in, remember the time you burned down my apartment?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (To Monica) Yeah, you're on your own.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Y'know what we should do? We should all get dressed up and go to have champagne at The Plaza.
They all agree and start to go and get ready.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: But I-I-I can't stay too long, I gotta get up early for a commercial audition tomorrow and I gotta look good. I'm supposed to be playing a 19-year-old. (Everyone stops in their tracks upon hearing this.) What?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: So when you said, "Get up early," did you mean 1986?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: You guys don't think I look 19?
A brief silence ensues.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, 19! We thought you said 90!
They all politely laugh and stop just as quickly.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Okay everybody, let's go! Let's go!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Okay.
They all leave and Joey comes back in quickly.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (To Phoebe) Hey, is uh the rest of my candy bar around here?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh honey no, you ate it all.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I was afraid of that.
Walks out and after he closes the door Phoebe turns around and takes a bite out of what is left of his candy bar.
Monica and Chandler's bedroom, they're getting ready.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (looking at her hand) Y'know what shoes would look great with this ring? Diamond shoes! (Sees Chandler sitting on the bed.) You're not getting dressed. (Chandler quietly folds over the comforter on the bed making a spot for her.)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Know what I mean?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yeah, but I don't think we have time.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: There's gonna be a wedding. You're gonna be the bride. Two hundred people are going to be looking at you in a clean white dress.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (lustily) Let's do it! (She kisses him and they fall back onto the bed.)
Monica, Chandler, and Phoebe's, time lapse, Chandler is fully dressed and slowly walking out of the bedroom with a distressed look on his face.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (chasing after him) Chandler! It happens to lots of guys! You-you-you were probably tired, you had a lot of champagne, don't worry about it!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (motioning with his hands) I'm not worried, I'm uh, I'm fascinated. Y'know it's like uh, Biology! Which is funny because in high school I uh, I-I failed Biology and tonight Biology failed me. (Exits as Phoebe enters from her room with her guitar.)

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Check it out. Okay, I can play this when the guests are coming in. Okay. (Singing)
"First time I met Chandler, I thought he was gay.
But here I am singing on his wedding day!"

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Phoebe!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: If you would've let me finish, it goes on to say that he's probably not gay.
Joey and Rachel's, Chandler is looking at the foosball table.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Sure, you guys don't have this problem, you're made of wood. (Rachel comes out of the bathroom) Hey!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: You look great!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (quietly) Oh, thanks.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: You okay over there?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I don't know, y'know? I feel a little umm... No, y'know what? Nevermind, I'm gonna be fine.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh, don't worry about it I mean you probably were tired, you had a lot of champagne, it happens to everybody.
[Rachel exits into the hallway just as Ross is coming up the stairs.]

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hi!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Wow! Happy Monica's night!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well thank you, you too.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Thanks.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hey, do you believe this? Do you believe they are actually getting married?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Well sure. But I get married all the time so...

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ohh...

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: You okay?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah, I guess. I-I... I mean, do-do you think we're ever gonna have that?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: You mean, we-you and me?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh no-no-no-no-no, no, no! We, you with someone and me with someone.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh good, you scared me for a minute.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Shake it off.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I mean-no, it's just 'cause, it's just 'cause you and I were like a nightmare. (Screams.) No, but there was some good times.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No, absolutely. Y'know like it was umm...

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Surely you can think of something good.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah, just give me a minute! (Thinking) Oh well, yes, I can think of one good thing.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well you uh, you were always really good at the uh, at the uh the stuff.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah? I was good at the stuff huh?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Uh-hmm, uh-hmm, yeah, yeah, I really liked your hands.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: My hands?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Uh-huh.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (to his hands) Way to go guys. Y'know, you-you were really good at the stuff too.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, I know. Hey, y'know what we never did? (Ross looks at her.) Oh no, not that. (Ross nods okay.) We uh, we never had bonus night!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: A what?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Y'know, bonus night. Y'know, when two people break up but they get back together for just one night.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: One night, just-just sex. No strings attached?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah-yeah, we never had that,

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No.
[Silence.]

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Okay, this is getting a little crazy. I mean, I'm-I'm sure it would be amazing but I...gotta say I really-really don't think it would be a good idea. Y'know? I really, really...don't.

Joey and Rachel's, Chandler is playing Playstation, Crash Team Racing to be exact (he's in last on Hot Air Skyway to be more exact) as Joey enters from his room desperately trying to look like a 19-year-old. He's got the wool cap, he's got the cut-off Knicks jersey over the faded T-shirt, and he's got the whole pants-around-the-knees-showing-off-the-boxers thing that rich, white, suburban kids have adopted in a desperate and extremely futile attempt to try to look like they're from the inner-city.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: 'Sup? 'Sup dude?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (putting his hands up) Take whatever you want, just please don't hurt me.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: So you're playing a little Playstation, huh? That's whack! Playstation is whack! 'Sup with the whack Playstation, 'sup?! Huh? Come on, am I 19 or what?!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yes, on a scale from 1 to 10, 10 being the dumbest a person can look, you are definitely 19.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Come on man, really how old?!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Young! You're a man-child okay?! Now go get changed because everybody's ready and please, oh please, keep my underwear!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Wow thanks! (He goes into his bedroom and closes the door.)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Joe?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Uhh, you've had a lot of sex right?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: When? Today? Some, not a lot.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well, it's just the reason that I'm asking is because I kind of eh, uh, I was unable to-I mean I really wanted too, but I couldn't.... There huh-hmm, there-there was an incident.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Don't worry about that man, that happens.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: It's happened to you?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah! Once.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well, what'd you do?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I did it anyway.
Monica, Chandler, and Phoebe's, Monica is in the living room and Phoebe is in her room.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Phoebe! Come on! Let's go! (Knocks on her door.) Come on! (Phoebe enters with guitar and not ready to go.) Why aren't you dressed yet?!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I'm sorry, but I just wrote the best dance song for your wedding. Check this out. (Gets ready to play.)

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (grabs the guitar away from her) No, Phoebe, I'll tell you what, if you get ready now I'll let you play it at the wedding.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Really?! Oh that's so exciting! Thank you! Thanks Mon! Oh but Mon, if you touch my guitar again I'll have to pound on you for a little bit.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Fair enough, now go get ready!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay. (She goes to get ready.)

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I'll get everybody else (Does so), finally we can start celebrating my-(She gasps and is stopped by the sight of Ross and Rachel making out in the hall.) I'm sorry, uh apparently I've opened the door to the past.
Commercial Break
Monica, Chandler, and Phoebe's, continued from before the commercial break. Monica is walking into the apartment followed by Ross and Rachel.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Okay, Monica. Mon, uh what-what you just saw...

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (interrupting) Can I ask you just a little question, huh? Why tonight?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: See, I've been waiting my whole life to be engaged, and unlike some people I'm only planning on doing this once. So, uh y'know, maybe this is selfish and I'm sorry about it, but I was kinda hoping tonight could just be about that.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh honey, but it is just about...

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: It is! It is!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: No it's not! No! No! Now it's about you and Ross getting back together!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: What?!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: See yeah umm, you kinda stole my thunder!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Okay! Ho-ho! We did not steal your thunder because we are not getting back together!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah. No. And you know what? Nobody even saw!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: That's true.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Honey I swear it we just kissed.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: It was just a kiss. (Phoebe enters and overhears this.)

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: You guys kissed!!!!! What does this mean?!! Are you, are you getting back together?! Can I sing at your wedding?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Thunder being stolen!!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay come on Phoebe, it's nothing! Monica, come on!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Look let's not make a big deal out of this! It was a one time thing. It doesn't even matter!
Chandler and Joey enter and overhear that.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh my God! I cannot believe you guys are talking about this! The problems in the bedroom are between the man and the woman!!! All right?!! Now Chandler is doing the best he can!!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (angrily) I don't think that's what they were talking about Joe!!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: What a great night, Chandler can't do it, these guys kissed... (Points to Ross and Rachel.)

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: What?!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: You guys kissed! Oh my-this is huge!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh no!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No-no, it's really not huge.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: And; people thinking it's huge has led Monica to believe that we are stealing her thunder. (To Monica) Which we are not!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Well, we're still talking about it, aren't we?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Well yeah, that and Chandler's problem.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Monica-Monica-Monica-Monica, listen-listen, listen, listen, would-would it make you feel better if we all stop talking about Ross and Rachel.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yes that would be lovely.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: You got it. Okay. Now, I can pass for 19 right?!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yes, you can pass for 19.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Really?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yes!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Seriously?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Seriously? Seriously, no! You can play your own age which is 31!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (gasps) I'm 30!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Joey, you are not! You're 31.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (realizes) Aww crap!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay, so The Plaza! Okay, we'll get us some Mai Thai's, (To Chandler) maybe no more for you though.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Y'know what? I-I think that umm, I don't feel like going to The Plaza.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Why?!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Honey, Monica, this is ridiculous! Look...

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: No-no, I-I really don't want to talk about it! I don't! (To Rachel) Especially with you. (Goes into her room.)

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Psss, that is whack!
[Time lapse, Phoebe is playing the guitar for Joey.]

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (she's strumming something) Yeah? (Joey nods yes.) Okay, I think I'll play it at the wedding.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah! Well, I think we'll see if they actually let you play. Huh? I mean they tell you anything you want to hear like-like, "You look 19," and then they just take it away like-like, "No you don't."

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Well, I don't think Monica is gonna take this away.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Wouldn't she?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Would she?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Would she? (He smells something and gasps as he realizes what it is.) You ate my candy bar!
Rachel's bedroom, she's pacing as Ross knocks on her door and opens it a little to stick his hands in.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Guess who? (Enters fully.)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hey.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey, I just realized we kinda let some stuff up in the air...

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: What do you think Monica mean when she said she didn't want to talk, especially with me? I mean, why not especially you and me? We were both out there kissing.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Still thinking about it huh?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Come on! Serious-ser-ser-seriously, what did she mean by that? (Mimicking Monica.) Especially you!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (loosening his tie) Oh, who cares?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I care!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (tightening his tie) And so do I.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Y'know what, I-I have to go talk to her, would you let me just get changed?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Okay. Sure. (Sits down on her bed.)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Am I going to let you watch me undress?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (sitting up) No! (Exits.)
Monica and Chandler's bedroom, they are both getting undressed.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I can't believe her, y'know it's just-it's so typical.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Now Monica, I know you're upset, but don't forget. There is going to be a wedding, you are going to throw the bouquet, and then there's going to be a honeymoon, maybe in Paris.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Paris?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: We will take a moonlit walk on the Rue de la (mumbles something).

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Keep talking.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Then we will sprinkle rose pedals on the bed and make love. Not just because it's romantic, but because I can!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I love you!
They start making out and both start to feel something growing below the belt line.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (in a French accent) Bonjour, monsieur.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Okay, don't say anything, you might scare it away.
There is a knock on the door.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: It's Paris, who knows we're here!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (opening the door) Hi Pheebs, what's up? (She enters.)

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay, you said I could sing at your wedding so, I'm just gonna need a small deposit.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What?!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Y'know, just some good faith money to hold the date.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Pheebs, we're not giving you a deposit for our wedding!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, I see. (Exits angrily.)
[Cut to the living room, Joey is eating a sandwich.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: They break your heart, don't they?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Y'know, I don't really their permission.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah! If you wanna sing at their wedding, well you sing at their wedding!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah! And if you wanna look 19, then you... You gotta do something about your eyes.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: What?! What's wrong with my eyes.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: They give you away! There's just-there's just too much wisdom in there. (Joey nods in agreement.) Just put some tea bags on there for like 15 minutes.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: And that'll get rid of my wisdom?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Maybe just 10 minutes for you.
[Cut back to Monica and Chandler's room, they are making out again as there's another knock on the door.]

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh, give her the deposit! Give her the ring! I don't care!
Monica opens the door to Ross and Rachel.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yes.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Monica, what did you mean before when you said you didn't want to talk to anyone, especially me?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: What a great apology! (To Monica) And you accept! Okay, bye-bye!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No-no, seriously-seriously, what was the especially me part about?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Well, let's just say it's not the first time you've stolen my thunder.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: What?!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (To Rachel) Hey, here's a thought.
Rachel ignores him and follows Monica into the kitchen.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Monica, what are you talking about?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: My Sweet Sixteen! Remember, you went to third base with my cousin Charlie.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (entering) Ahh, third base.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: It's all everybody at the party could talk about!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Monica, y'know what? The only reason I did that was because your party was so boring!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (gasps) We had a characturist!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh!!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (singing, angrily)
"Whenever I get married, guess who won't get to sing?
Somebody named Geller! And somebody else named Bing!"
Exits.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Monica, your Sweet Sixteen was like a million years ago.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: And yet, here we are doing it again.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ugh, Monica I don't want to steal your stupid thunder!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh please! Why else would you have made out with Ross?!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Got me. (He shows of his hands.)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: All right, easy mimey, the moment has passed, it ain't gonna happen!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I just thought it would be nice if I could have just this one night!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I swear, I never wanted any part of your night!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh, is that why you did it the secret hallway where nobody ever goes?!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Uh, Rachel, I've been thinking. I don't think us getting together tonight is such a good idea. I'm calling it off.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (To Ross) Why to save your dignity my man.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (ignoring them) Monica, why? Why would I ever want to take away from your night?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I don't know! I don't-maybe you're feeling a little resentful. Maybe ah, maybe you thought you'd get married first! Maybe you can't stand the fact that your formally fat friend is getting married before you!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh wow. That-y'know what? That is so unfair. Y'know what? Now I want to steal your thunder! Come on Ross, let's go have sex!
Ross tries to dismiss it by making that sound, but decides to go for it and follows Rachel.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (yelling after them) I can't believe you're gonna have sex on my engagement night!!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well, somebody should. (Monica glares at him.)
[Cut to Rachel's bedroom.]

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Look uh, if we're gonna do this...

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: We're not gonna do this, all right? She's just gonna think that we're doin' it.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh, I see, so everybody wins.
There's a knock on the door.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Who is it?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (outside the door) It's Monica, open up!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay well Ross! Stop it please! Wait a minute! (Motions for him to follow her lead, but he angrily shakes his head no. So she pokes him.)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oww!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah, you like that baby? (Monica bursts in followed by Chandler.) May we help you?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I just wanted to say that I hope you do have sex tonight and I hope that you guys get back together, but I must warn you, the night that you announce your engagement I'm going to announce that I'm pregnant!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: How is that ever going to happen?!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: All right Monica, do you want to know why I was with Ross tonight?!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I know why!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No you don't know why!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Okay! Why?!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Because! Because I was sad.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What do you mean?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Look, I am so...so happy for you guys, but you getting married just reminds me of the fact that I'm not. I'm not even close. And I don't know, maybe I just wanted to make myself feel better. And I know that that's dumb, but oh my God you were so depressed when Ross got married that you slept with Chandler!
Ross looks at Chandler.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (To Ross) I don't care, she slept with me.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Anyway sweetie, I am, I'm so sorry I ruined your night.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (starting to cry) I'm sorry I almost made you sleep with Ross. (They hug.)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (deadpan) Well, I'm going to take off. (To Chandler) Congratulations man.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Thanks.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (at the door) And uh, Rachel.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What can I say, you missed your chance. From now on the only person who's going to enjoy these bad boys (holds up his hands) is me. (Quickly realizes what he said and exits disgustedly.)
Ending Credits
Monica, Chandler, and Phoebe's, Phoebe is singing outside Monica and Chandler's door.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (singing)
"We thought Phoebe would leave, but she just stayed and stayed.
That's right, I'm here all night, and Chandler will never get l..."

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (interrupting her just in time) Hey! Here's a dollar, consider it a deposit. Please sing at our wedding.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh thank you.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Okay. (Goes back to bed.)

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Now... (Starts singing again) "Who will perform the ceremony! Who will perform the cer-(Chandler enters and grabs her guitar and closes the door behind him)-Oh-oh! All right, I'll pound on him in the morning.
End
